specification:
Type: microsim

    Learning objective: Help students visualize their emergency fund journey, set appropriate targets, and track progress toward financial security (Bloom's: Applying, Creating)

    Canvas layout (900x650px):
    - Left side (600x650): Visual progress roadmap
    - Right side (300x650): Calculator and inputs

    Visual elements:

    Progress Roadmap (left panel):
    - Vertical path with 4 milestone markers:
      1. START: "No Emergency Fund" (bottom, gray)
      2. MILESTONE 1: "$500-1,000 Starter Fund" (checkered flag icon)
      3. MILESTONE 2: "3-Month Emergency Fund" (bronze shield icon)
      4. MILESTONE 3: "6-Month Emergency Fund" (gold shield icon)

    - Progress bar fills from bottom to top as user enters data
    - Current position marked with animated character climbing the path
    - Celebratory animations when milestones reached
    - Dollar amounts shown at each milestone
    - Time estimates shown: "X months to next milestone at current savings rate"

    Right Panel - Calculator:

    Section 1: Essential Monthly Expenses
    Input fields with $ inputs:
    - Housing (rent/mortgage): $____
    - Utilities: $____
    - Groceries: $____
    - Transportation: $____
    - Insurance: $____
    - Minimum debt payments: $____
    - Other essentials: $____
    - Auto-calculate: "Total Essential Expenses: $____/month"

    Section 2: Emergency Fund Targets
    Auto-calculated based on essential expenses:
    - Starter Fund: $500-1,000
    - 3-Month Fund: $____ (3 × monthly essentials)
    - 6-Month Fund: $____ (6 × monthly essentials)

    Section 3: Current Status
    - Current emergency fund balance: $____
    - Monthly savings amount: $____
    - Progress percentage: ___%
    - Current milestone: [name]

    Section 4: Timeline Projection
    Auto-calculated:
    - "At $X/month savings rate:"
    - Days to starter fund: ___ days
    - Months to 3-month fund: ___ months
    - Months to 6-month fund: ___ months
    - Date you'll reach 6-month fund: [date]

    Section 5: Boost Tools
    "Accelerate your timeline:"
    - Button: "Add one-time windfall" (tax refund, bonus, gift)
      - Input: Amount $____
      - Shows updated timeline
    - Button: "Increase monthly savings by $25"
      - Shows impact on timeline
    - Slider: Test different savings rates to see impact

    Interactive features:
    - Real-time calculation as user types
    - "Save My Progress" - stores data in browser
    - "Share My Goal" - generates shareable image
    - Scenario buttons:
      - "Typical college student" - pre-fills realistic values
      - "Young professional" - different preset
      - "Family household" - higher expenses preset

    Visual feedback:
    - Green glow when milestone reached
    - Progress bar fills with gradient (red→yellow→green)
    - Confetti animation when completing major milestones
    - Achievement badges earned at each level

    Educational insights displayed:
    - "Great start! A $1,000 buffer prevents most small emergencies."
    - "You're 23% of the way to full protection!"
    - "At your current rate, you'll have complete peace of mind by [date]."
    - "Increasing savings by just $50/month cuts your timeline by X months!"
    - "Remember: This is for EMERGENCIES only, not wants or planned expenses."

    Emergency scenarios (educational examples):
    - Click "?" icons to see example scenarios:
      - Car repair $800 → Covered by starter fund
      - Job loss 4 months → Covered by 6-month fund
      - Medical emergency $1,200 → Covered by 3-month fund
      - Multiple emergencies at once → Shows why 6 months is better

    Implementation: p5.js for visual roadmap and progress bar, JavaScript for calculations, local storage for saving progress
